headline: Transcript: Sequoia Capital’s free speech debate
summary: Marc Filippino talks to Stephen Morris and Eva Xiao
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the FT News Briefing podcast episode: ‘Sequoia Capital’s free speech debate’

Marc FilippinoGood morning from the Financial Times. Today is Thursday, October 23rd, and this is your FT News Briefing.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

Tesla’s profits took a major hit last quarter and a Sequoia Capital executive quit over a contentious free speech issue. Plus, the Trump administration has been quietly suppressing government climate change data.

I’m Marc Filippino, and here’s the news you need to start your day.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

Tesla saw its profits drop nearly 30 per cent last quarter. The electric car company reported earnings yesterday and it said that its adjusted net income fell to $1.8bn that just missed Wall Street expectations. Tesla pointed to the cost associated with its pivot to robotics and artificial intelligence.
